message:
feat: Add dependency graph and impact analysis commands

New CLI Commands:
- jfp impact <prompt-id>: Show which bundles and workflows depend on a prompt
  Useful for understanding downstream effects before modifying a prompt
- jfp graph export: Export the full prompt dependency graph
  Returns nodes (prompts, bundles, workflows) and edges showing relationships

Implementation:
- packages/cli/src/commands/impact.ts: Find bundles containing a prompt and
  workflows that reference it in their steps
- packages/cli/src/commands/graph.ts: Build full dependency graph with typed
  nodes and edges for visualization or analysis tools

CLI Integration:
- Added "Analysis" section to help.ts with new commands
- Added examples for impact and graph export commands
- Registered both commands in index.ts

Tests:
- packages/cli/__tests__/commands/impact.test.ts
- packages/cli/__tests__/commands/graph.test.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -3,11 +3,11 @@ {"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts. ## Scope - Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI). - UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs. - Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts. - CLI: `jfp cost ` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility. - UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts. - CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging. - Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Cost calculation across models and price tiers. - Budget threshold logic and alert formatting. ### Integration Tests - Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output. - Budget updates persist and are read by CLI. ### E2E Tests - Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI. ## Success Criteria - Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage. - Users can see cost before running prompts. - Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:44:54.324677012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-2kwr","title":"Fix support ticket rate limiting - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates - Items appear in sidebar after viewing ## Files to Modify - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests) - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors) ## Success Criteria - Mobile & Desktop Optimization","description":"## Overview Hyper-optimize both mobile and desktop UI/UX to achieve world-class visual appeal, polish, and slickness following best practices from industry leaders like Stripe. ## Completed Work 1. ✅ Fixed critical mobile button text truncation in PromptDetailModal - Proper safe area insets for notched devices - Bottom-sheet interactions for modals - Optimized category filter horizontal scrolling - Touch-friendly gestures with haptic feedback ## Desktop Optimization Goals - Refined hover states and micro-interactions - Proper keyboard navigation - Consistent typography and spacing - Premium shadow and elevation design - Stripe-level polish on all interactive elements ## Components to Audit - [x] PromptDetailModal - Fixed - [x] Button component - Already well-designed - [x] BottomSheet - Well-designed with safe area support - [x] SpotlightSearch - Well-designed with mobile-first approach - [x] Nav - Good touch targets - [x] BottomNav - Good mobile design - [x] PromptCard - Good Stripe-inspired design ## Status Initial critical bug fixed. Pro users need to understand what will break if a prompt changes. ## Scope - Maintain graph edges: prompt -> packs, workflows, schedules, share links, and collections. - Impact analysis when editing: show downstream items and required confirmations. - Graph view with filters and export. - CLI: `jfp impact ` and `jfp graph export`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Graph storage model + API endpoints. - UI graph explorer + impact warnings. - CLI commands with JSON export. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Graph integrity rules and edge updates on edits/deletes. - Impact analysis accuracy. ### Integration Tests - Changes in prompts propagate to dependent items. - Graph export format is stable. ### E2E Tests - Edit prompt -> impact modal -> confirm -> updates applied. - CLI impact -> matches UI counts. ## Success Criteria - Users can see downstream dependencies before editing. - Accidental breaking changes are prevented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:14.088091888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:14.088091888Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-d43s","title":"Feature: Prompt Dependency Graph + Impact Analysis","description":"## Background Prompts are reused across packs, workflows, schedules, and share links. Pro users need to understand what will break if a prompt changes. ## Scope - Maintain graph edges: prompt -> packs, workflows, schedules, share links, and collections. - Impact analysis when editing: show downstream items and required confirmations. - Graph view with filters and export. - CLI: `jfp impact ` and `jfp graph export`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Graph storage model + API endpoints. - UI graph explorer + impact warnings. - CLI commands with JSON export. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Graph integrity rules and edge updates on edits/deletes. - Impact analysis accuracy. ### Integration Tests - Changes in prompts propagate to dependent items. - Graph export format is stable. ### E2E Tests - Edit prompt -> impact modal -> confirm -> updates applied. - CLI impact -> matches UI counts. ## Success Criteria - Users can see downstream dependencies before editing. - Accidental breaking changes are prevented.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:14.088091888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:39:45.332628763Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-kfuj","title":"Feature: CLI Premium Pack Subscriptions + Update Notifications","description":"## Background Pro users subscribe to premium prompt packs and expect updates to flow into their CLI without manual downloads. ## Scope - CLI pack commands: `jfp packs subscribe/list/update/changelog`. - Local cache with signed manifest and version history. - Update notifications on new pack releases; optional auto‑update. - License metadata + entitlement checks (Pro-only). - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Pack subscription API endpoints + entitlement checks. - CLI flows for subscribe/update and changelog viewing. - Local cache format + integrity verification. - Upgrade messaging for free users. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Manifest verification and cache integrity. - Entitlement checks and error formatting. ### Integration Tests - Subscribe -> update -> changelog with real API calls. - Cache refresh on new pack release. ### E2E Tests - Pro user subscribes -> pack content available in CLI. - Free user attempt -> upgrade guidance. ## Success Criteria - Pro users can keep packs up to date with minimal effort. - Updates are secure and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:29.605565630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:29.605565630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-wsye","title":"Docs: Clarify CLI Free vs Pro + Build Guidance","description":"## Background Users reported that the jfp CLI does not compile and are confused about Free vs Pro gating. ## Scope - Clarify Free vs Pro command gating in the main README. - Add build guidance confirming no Pro account is required to compile. - Update CLI premium integration doc with a gating summary. - Improve bug report template for build failures. ## Completed Changes - Updated README Free vs Pro section + build steps. - Added gating summary to docs/cli-premium-integration.md. - Added CLI build diagnostics section to .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md. ## Success Criteria - OSS users can build the CLI without Pro. - Pro-only commands are clearly communicated. - Issue reports include build details. ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:57:57.737302847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T13:57:57.737302847Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T13:57:57.737302847Z","external_ref":"bd-pg3a","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-02u","title":"Build ChangelogAccordion for prompt detail pages","description":"**Details:** - Shows version history when prompt.changelog exists - Accordion expansion (collapsed by default) - Lists changes chronologically (newest first) - Version, date, and change description per entry **Changelog Type (in types.ts):** ```typescript interface PromptChange { version: string; date: string; changes: string[]; } // On Prompt interface: changelog?: PromptChange[]; ``` **Implementation:** ```typescript // apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx interface ChangelogAccordionProps { changelog: PromptChange[]; } export function ChangelogAccordion({ changelog }: ChangelogAccordionProps) { const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false); if (!changelog?.length) return null; return (
{expanded && (
    {changelog.map((entry) => (
  • {entry.version} {entry.date}
      {entry.changes.map((change, i) => (
    • {change}
      • ))}
    • ))}
)}
); } ``` **Location:** apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx **Reasoning:** Changelogs build trust and help users understand prompt evolution. Accordion keeps the UI clean while making history accessible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:35:24.004177827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:50:21.229495631Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:50:21.229495631Z","close_reason":"Created ChangelogAccordion with collapsible UI, type-specific icons/colors, and timeline styling.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
